data.title: NVIDIA unveils memory optimization for Jetson, freeing up 12GB for 10B parameter AI models on edge devices.
data.description: NVIDIA has unveiled new optimization techniques that can free up to 12GB of memory on Jetson devices. This enables the smooth running of multi-billion parameter Large Language Models (LLMs) on edge hardware. This advancement in optimization allows for more efficient operation of these powerful models on the edge, making them more accessible and practical for a wider range of applications.

data.title: New NVIDIA NeMo RL training method achieves 48% speedup using End-to-End FP8 precision, reducing infrastructure costs.
data.description: NVIDIA has introduced a new FP8 formula for reinforcement learning that leads to a 48% increase in training speed compared to previous methods, while maintaining the same accuracy level as BF16. This innovation offers a substantial reduction in AI infrastructure expenses.
Asia-Pacific stocks rise as geopolitical sentiment stabilizes, driven by expectations though still uncertain of renewed US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad.

data.title: Inland Empire Health Plan ranks in 100th percentile on provider satisfaction survey
data.description: In Rancho Cucamonga, California, on April 20, 2026, the Inland Empire Health Plan (IEHP), ranked among the top five largest Medicaid health plans in the United States, has secured a perfect score in overall satisfaction on Press Ganey's Provider Experience Survey, placing them at the 100th percentile nationally. This achievement is a significant milestone for IEHP, highlighting their commitment to excellence in healthcare delivery.

data.title: Greenberg Traurig's Jane McLaughlin Selected for City & State's 2026 'Who's Who in Energy' List
data.description: In Albany, New York, on April 20, 2026, Jane E. McLaughlin, a member of global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Albany office, has been recognized in the 2026 City & State "Who's Who in Energy" list. This prestigious distinction showcases influential individuals involved in the energy sector.

data.title: AMC Robotics Reports Full Year 2025 Financial Results
data.description: On April 20, 2026, AMC Robotics Corporation (Nasdaq: AMCI), a leading provider of AI-powered robotics solutions, announced its financial results for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2025. The company shared its performance and achievements during this period.

data.title: The Many Acquires Majority Stake in Immersive Technology Studio CatalystXR, Expanding Global Participation Capabilities
data.description: In Los Angeles, The Many, an independent agency renowned for developing engagement-centered brands, has revealed the purchase of a controlling interest in CatalystXR, an Australian immersive technology firm dedicated to XR, VR, AR, and extensive interactive settings. As part of the deal, CatalystXR's founder, Jamie Gilroy, will assume the role of Partner overseeing Participatory Technology at The Many. This move signifies a significant step in The Many's worldwide growth, solidifying its presence across offices in various locations.

data.title: Traction Uranium Engages Market Maker
data.description: Traction Uranium Corp. has chosen Independent Trading Group, Inc. as the market maker for its common shares on the Canadian Securities Exchange. The company's stock, listed as CSE: TRAC, OTC: TRCTF, and FRA: Z1K, will now be managed by ITG, located at 420, 33 Yonge Street, Toronto, ON, Canada, M5E 1G4, with contact person Chris Kaplan reachable via chriskaplan@itg84.com.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico Announces Results for the First Quarter of 2026
data.description: In Guadalajara, Mexico, on April 20, 2026, Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V. (NYSE: PAC; BMV: GAP), known as GAP, released its unaudited consolidated results for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2026 (1Q26), based on International Financial Reporting Standards from the International Accounting Standards Board. The figures provided do not include the recent business combination approved on December 11, 2025, involving the integration of Cross Border Xpress (CBX) and internalization of technical assistance services from AMP. The definitive agreements pertaining to this transaction have not been finalized, and closing the deal is contingent upon meeting standard closing conditions.
